title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will play against the Washington Wizards on Saturday after missing a game for injury management.
Tatum debuted this season on March 6 after recovering from a ruptured right Achilles sustained 10 months ago.
Celtics players Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic is out against the Wizards.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' featuring Alan Ritchson is now streaming on Netflix, spotlighting a soldier's journey through PTSD during Army Ranger training.
Disney's 'Zootopia 2' with Nick and Judy tackling a new mystery is available on Disney+ before the 2026 Oscars.
'One Piece' Season 2, focusing on pirate adventures in the Grand Line, is streaming on Netflix.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots signed former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker to strengthen offensive line after trading center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears.
Vera-Tucker, 26, missed all of last season due to a triceps injury and has a history of injuries, including an Achilles tear.
Former Jets teammate Morgan Moses helped persuade Vera-Tucker to join the Patriots, emphasizing the team's winning culture and strong coaching staff.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora names Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as leaders of the 2026 Boston Red Sox.
Trevor Story led Red Sox batters in home runs, RBIs, runs, and hits in the previous season.
Garrett Crochet was MLB's strikeout leader in 2025 and is compared to legendary pitchers by Cora.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC will make its NWSL debut against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
Head coach Filipa Patão plans an attacking style for the 14 players making their NWSL debut.
Legacy FC will play on newly-installed grass at Gillette Stadium, suited for their possession-style game.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are considering legislation to stop changing clocks twice a year, possibly adopting a permanent time system.
Senate President Karen E. Spilka supports bill S.2157, suggesting permanent Atlantic Standard Time to improve health and productivity.
Research links time changes to health issues like heart attacks; the proposal is under review by the Senate rules committee.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announced the 2026 Pride celebration will take place on Saturday, June 6.
The event features a parade from Copley Square to Boston Common, followed by a festival and block party.
Mayor Michelle Wu praised the organization's work to promote inclusivity and joy in the LGBTQ+ community.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ordered the release of a 14-year-old girl detained by ICE in Marlborough, Massachusetts.
The Brazilian national was detained by ICE while with alleged gang members and later released to her aunt in Boston.
The girl has been given a notice to appear in immigration court, potentially facing deportation from the U.S.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins have struggled with scoring, holding onto the final wild-card spot by one point.
Pastrnak is producing points but mostly outside 5-on-5 play; Lindholm and Geekie have zero 5-on-5 points recently.
Coach Marco Sturm considers a lineup reshuffle after recent losses, but kept lines the same during Friday practice.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a Swiss student, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise $10,000 for the Stepping Strong Center.
Cogne is a visiting researcher at Harvard Medical School, focusing on neurorehabilitation for trauma survivors.
His marathon run aims to support research and patient programs at Brigham and Women’s Hospital.
